classDescription: A swaggering raider, duelist, and sea-scoundrel who wins through mobility, pressure, dirty tricks, and presence.
roleIdentity: Dexterity/Charisma martial controller who mixes movement, debuffs, leadership, and opportunistic burst damage.

pirate-sea-legs.summary: Your movement, balance, and footing stay reliable on unstable ground, rigging, and decks.
pirate-sea-legs.description: Sea Legs is the class foundation. A Pirate should feel immediately harder to pin down in messy spaces: gangplanks, ropes, rocking decks, tavern tables, and cluttered battlefields. Even before the class becomes flashy, the player should feel like they belong in motion.

pirate-swagger-dice.summary: Gain Swagger Dice, a renewable pool of daring that powers tricks, pressure, and leadership.
pirate-swagger-dice.description: Swagger Dice are the class resource and social signature. They represent nerve, bravado, timing, and the way a Pirate seizes a room or a fight. The sheet should surface them like a real spendable currency with obvious use buttons later attached to subclass and class tricks.

pirate-dirty-fighting.summary: Unlock dirty tricks that reward positioning, distraction, and mean little advantages.
pirate-dirty-fighting.description: Dirty Fighting is what keeps the Pirate from feeling like a generic rogue-fighter hybrid. The class should encourage opportunistic play: blind the mark, shove them into bad footing, yank them out of position, or set up the crew for a better turn.

pirate-cunning-footwork.summary: Move through threatened spaces and awkward angles more freely than most warriors.
pirate-cunning-footwork.description: This feature pushes the class into mobile skirmisher territory. It should read like clean, practical mobility rather than magic, helping the Pirate get to ladders, rails, cover, and flank lanes without the player feeling trapped.

pirate-boarding-action.summary: Explode into a fight with a burst of movement or pressure when you close the distance.
pirate-boarding-action.description: Boarding Action is a tempo feature. When the Pirate commits, the turn should feel dramatic and forward-moving, like a leap over a rail or a rush through smoke into the center of a melee.

pirate-pirate-calling-subclass.summary: Choose the style of Pirate you became: duelist, captain, infiltrator, or terror of the seas.
pirate-pirate-calling-subclass.description: Pirate Calling is the subclass gate that defines your fighting identity at level 3. Each calling takes the class's core swagger-and-footwork chassis in a different direction — a Dread Corsair specializes in fear and brutal pressure, a Captain of Fortune leads through inspiration and resource sharing, a Cutthroat thrives on ambush and dirty tricks, and a Sea Devil dominates aquatic and boarding combat. This choice shapes which tricks, movement options, and late-game capstone abilities you accumulate.

pirate-marked-quarry.summary: Single out a target and pressure them with relentless pursuit and opportunistic punishment.
pirate-marked-quarry.description: Marked Quarry gives the Pirate a target-priority engine. It is less about mystical hunting and more about reading the table, calling the shot, and making one enemy feel hunted by timing and presence.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-1.summary: Increase ability scores or take a feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-1.description: At level 4 the Pirate earns their first Ability Score Improvement. Strength or Dexterity are the primary targets since they power weapon attacks and AC. Charisma serves the class's social identity and some Calling features. Alternatively, feats like Tavern Brawler, Mobile, or Lucky complement the Pirate's mobile, opportunistic combat style especially well.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-2.summary: Increase ability scores or take a feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-2.description: At level 8 the Pirate gains a second ASI. If the primary attack stat isn't maxed yet this is the time to finish it. Alternatively, Constitution for more HP and Concentration durability, or a combat feat that adds a tactical layer to your Swagger Dice and Dirty Fighting options are strong investments at this level.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-3.summary: Increase ability scores or take a feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-3.description: At level 12 the Pirate gains a third ASI. By this point primary combat stats are usually maxed, making this a strong opportunity for a feat. Options like Alert (better initiative for opening-round advantage), Resilient (Wisdom for save defense), or Sentinel (controlling adjacent enemies) all serve a mid-tier Pirate well.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Increase ability scores or take a feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-4.description: At level 16 the Pirate gains their fourth and final standard ASI. At this stage the character should be fully realized mechanically, making this a natural slot for a feat that adds a unique high-level signature ability rather than further stat improvements. Epic Boon eligibility is one level away so choose something that complements the final shape of the build.

pirate-extra-attack.summary: Attack twice whenever you take the Attack action.
pirate-extra-attack.description: Extra Attack at level 5 is the most important offensive milestone for the Pirate. Two attacks per action means consistent damage output and more opportunities to proc Dirty Fighting triggers, Swagger Dice effects, and Marked Quarry bonuses on the same turn. This level is where the Pirate's action economy starts to feel decisively stronger than most opponents.
pirate-extra-attack.effect: Make 2 attacks whenever you take the Attack action.

pirate-swagger-dice-d8.summary: Your Swagger Dice improve to d8s, making your tricks and support sharper.
pirate-swagger-dice-d8.description: At level 5, your Swagger Dice upgrade from d6s to d8s. Every Swagger Die you spend on tricks, support, or pressure now delivers a stronger result. This upgrade is automatic — all existing uses of Swagger Dice simply become more powerful, making all your level 1–4 tricks retroactively better without changing how they work.

pirate-no-quarter.summary: Capitalize on fear and disarray when an enemy is already under pressure.
pirate-no-quarter.description: No Quarter gives the class a vicious midgame identity. Once a Pirate has momentum, they should feel excellent at turning hesitation into collapse, especially against wounded, frightened, or cornered foes.

pirate-uncanny-balance.summary: Stay upright, mobile, and dangerous even in terrain that should slow everyone else down.
pirate-uncanny-balance.description: Uncanny Balance at level 7 is the Pirate's signature mobility mastery. Slippery decks, ice, difficult terrain, ropes, narrow ledges, and unstable footing that knock other characters prone or force Dexterity saves barely register for you. In fights where terrain is a factor — and for a Pirate, terrain always matters — this feature means you operate at full effectiveness while enemies are compromised.

pirate-menacing-reputation.summary: Your name starts affecting scenes before the fight even begins.
pirate-menacing-reputation.description: This is the class’s social inflection point. A veteran Pirate should not only win on a map; they should bend negotiations, intimidation, and morale by showing up with a reputation that matters.

pirate-subclass-feature-10.summary: Gain your next major subclass feature.
pirate-subclass-feature-10.description: At level 10 your Pirate Calling delivers a significant mid-tier ability that deepens your combat identity. A Dread Corsair becomes harder to escape and more terrifying in sustained combat, a Captain of Fortune gains stronger leadership tools, a Cutthroat gains more devastating ambush capabilities, or a Sea Devil gains advanced aquatic and boarding dominance. This feature is the second major architectural piece of your calling's kit.

pirate-press-the-advantage.summary: When your crew gains momentum, chain that advantage into extra pressure or support.
pirate-press-the-advantage.description: This is the feature that makes the Pirate feel like a battlefield opportunist rather than a rotation class. A good turn should create the next good turn, whether that means more damage, a shove, a reposition, or a setup for an ally.

pirate-swagger-dice-d10.summary: Your Swagger Dice improve to d10s.
pirate-swagger-dice-d10.description: At level 13, your Swagger Dice upgrade from d8s to d10s — the highest die size in the class. Every trick, morale effect, and support ability powered by Swagger Dice now hits harder or reaches further. This is the final die upgrade and marks the point where the Pirate's signature resource is operating at full power.

pirate-master-of-the-boarding-line.summary: Own ladders, rails, gangways, and other narrow spaces where movement decides the fight.
pirate-master-of-the-boarding-line.description: Master of the Boarding Line at level 13 makes you extraordinarily difficult to counter in the cramped, vertical, and chaotic terrain that defines a Pirate's natural environment. Narrow corridors, ship rigging, ladders, gangplanks, and tight chokepoints become your domain — spaces where your movement superiority and balance give you advantages that heavily armored enemies simply cannot match.

pirate-legend-of-the-waves.summary: Your sheer confidence and seamanship let you dictate the flow of chaotic battles.
pirate-legend-of-the-waves.description: Legend of the Waves at level 14 is the point where the Pirate's reputation and mastery transcend ordinary skill. Your presence alone changes how enemies behave — your confidence radiates across the battlefield, your positioning is impossible to anticipate, and your ability to redirect chaos in your favor makes you a force multiplier for the entire party. This feature represents the full realization of the Pirate as a battlefield presence rather than just a fighter.

pirate-subclass-feature-15.description: At level 15 your Pirate Calling delivers its final and most powerful ability. This is the ultimate expression of your chosen calling — the feature that makes a Dread Corsair truly terrifying, a Captain of Fortune a genuine force multiplier, a Cutthroat an unstoppable assassination engine, or a Sea Devil a sovereign of the seas. This completes your subclass kit and sets up the class capstone at level 20.

pirate-dread-volley.summary: Unload a decisive flurry of attacks, shots, or thrown weapons when the moment breaks open.
pirate-dread-volley.description: At high level, the Pirate should finally get a legendary burst turn that feels like pistols, blades, thrown steel, and ruthless precision all going off in one cinematic sequence.

pirate-indomitable-swagger.summary: Refuse to fold when fear, pain, or bad odds should make lesser fighters break.
pirate-indomitable-swagger.description: Indomitable Swagger at level 18 is the Pirate's ultimate resilience feature. When the situation is grim, the odds are against you, or an effect should by all rights shut you down, this feature lets you push through on raw defiance and nerve. A legendary Pirate does not break — they adapt, they bluster, and they find the gap. This feature makes that fantasy mechanically real.

pirate-epic-boon.summary: Gain an Epic Boon — a legendary feat-level upgrade that marks entry into near-mythic capability.
pirate-epic-boon.description: Epic Boon at level 19 is the penultimate power unlock for the Pirate. Epic Boons are stronger than standard feats and represent abilities that cross into the realm of legendary achievement. For a Pirate this might manifest as extraordinary combat resilience, an ability that bends the rules of their signature swagger, or a permanent upgrade that rounds out the class into a complete and definitive combat identity.

pirate-king-of-the-black-flag.summary: Become the definitive pirate legend: impossible to ignore, hard to pin down, and deadly once you commit.
pirate-king-of-the-black-flag.description: King of the Black Flag is the final statement of the class. At this point the Pirate should feel like the center of a boarding scene or sea duel, combining leadership, panic, movement, and brutal opportunism into one finished fantasy.

future: Surface dirty tricks as actionable buttons in the Actions tab and map-side quick panel.
Add stronger ship-combat integrations for rigging, boarding, morale, and cannon-adjacent play.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers attack rolls, AC, and Swagger Dice save DCs
buildTips.secondaryReason: sharpens class features, social presence, and most subclass themes
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: you fight in medium armor at close range and need the HP to survive trades
